patch.value: Keep large modules maintainable via barrel re-export splits + explicit uncovered allowlist

Decisions taken while completing pm-mbdu (file splits) and pm-zwl7 (extension error surfacing): (1) BARREL RE-EXPORT pattern: extract cohesive leaf clusters from oversized files into sibling modules, then re-export from the original so NO consumer import site changes; the original imports FROM the new modules to avoid cycles. (2) COVERAGE MODEL: new split modules are deliberately NOT added to vitest.config.ts coverage 'include' (so they inherit their parent's ungated status); they MUST instead be registered in the explicit uncovered allowlist governance test in tests/integration/release-readiness-runtime.spec.ts ('keeps vitest coverage include list aligned with src ts modules'). The static-quality-gate alone does NOT catch a missing allowlist entry — only the full coverage gate does. (3) EXTENSION ERROR SURFACING: surface a handler's failure cause via a dedicated errorMessage field on CommandHandlerResult (NOT inside the warnings array) so the telemetry warning code stays generic while the human cause reaches stderr; telemetry's existing sanitizeString(captureLevel) redacts paths from outcome.error. (4) CHANGELOG is fully auto-generated by the pm-changelog package from CLOSED items — never hand-edit CHANGELOG.md; close items (with accurate type/tags so classifyItem routes them) then run 'pnpm changelog:pm' to regenerate.

value.author: codex-full-audit
value.text: Code-quality/security audit note 2026-05-25: tracked secret scan is clean and static quality passes. Sub-agent review still found pack/build hygiene and complexity follow-ups outside this safe slice: build does not clean stale dist artifacts before packing, several modules remain large/complex, and mutation output verbosity overlaps pm-ch59. No private telemetry host/token material was found in tracked files by scripts/check-secrets.mjs. Keep this item open for larger dist-clean/complexity work.

author: codex-code-quality-specialist

value.author: codex-code-quality-specialist
value.text: Code-quality specialist evidence 2026-05-25: repo size/complexity scan found no static-quality violations, but maintainability risk remains. Size snapshot: 141315 LOC across src/tests/docs/packages/plugins; largest files include tests/integration/cli.integration.spec.ts 6989 LOC, tests/unit/extension-loader.spec.ts 3339 LOC, src/cli/commands/update.ts 1936 LOC, src/core/extensions/loader.ts 1929 LOC, src/cli/main.ts 1895 LOC. jscpd scan (npx -y jscpd --min-lines 24 --min-tokens 120 --reporters json --output /tmp/pm-cli-jscpd src tests docs packages plugins) took 41.806s and found 5 clone clusters / 260 duplicated lines / 0.33%. Generated duplicates: plugin MCP wrappers and package runtime loaders. Hand-maintained duplicates to consider: src/core/extensions/extension-policy.ts:156-183 duplicated at 277-303, and src/cli/commands/history-redact.ts:532-558 duplicated with src/cli/commands/history-repair.ts:171-197. Recommended fix: keep generated duplicate allowlist explicit, extract shared override normalization and shared history rewrite lock/ownership helper, and lower static-quality thresholds once these known long files are split.

message: Record static complexity and duplicate scan findings

value.author: ci-quality-subagent
value.text: CI/test maintainability evidence 2026-05-25: latest scan confirms pm-3cbk should stay open. Long-file hotspots remain tests/integration/cli.integration.spec.ts 6989 LOC, tests/unit/extension-loader.spec.ts 3339 LOC, tests/unit/test-command.spec.ts 2029 LOC, src/cli/commands/update.ts 1936 LOC, src/core/extensions/loader.ts 1929 LOC, src/cli/main.ts 1895 LOC, and tests/integration/release-readiness-runtime.spec.ts 1894 LOC. static-quality-gate still passes under current thresholds (max_test_lines 7000, max_src_lines 3400, max_complexity 260), so there is no immediate gate violation, but the thresholds are permissive. Recommendation remains to split high-volume integration specs by workflow and migrate subprocess-heavy clusters to runCliInProcess with a small retained subprocess parity set; lower thresholds only after those known files are split.

value.text: Linking dedup extraction follow-up to branch fix/nightly-cross-platform-stability-and-extension-policy-dedup: extracting buildExtensionPolicyOverride helper within extension-policy.ts (single-file extraction, no coverage gate change because the file is already in the uncovered allowlist). Removes 25-line duplicate between normalizeExtensionPolicy/hydrateExtensionPolicy override loops.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Implementation evidence 2026-05-26: extracted buildExtensionPolicyOverride + collectExtensionPolicyOverrides helpers inside src/core/extensions/extension-policy.ts (single-file, no coverage-include change needed because the file is already in the uncovered allowlist). Removed 25-line duplicate between normalizeExtensionPolicy and hydrateExtensionPolicy override loops. The decision pattern is preserved: the existing barrel re-export structure is untouched; consumers continue to import {normalize,hydrate}ExtensionPolicy with no changes. Verified via extension-loader/settings-store/settings-validator/contracts-command tests (90 tests pass) and full release gate (coverage stays at 100%, static-quality gate passes).

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Slice merged 2026-05-26 via PR #73 (commit 60659285): buildExtensionPolicyOverride + collectExtensionPolicyOverrides extraction inside extension-policy.ts removed the 25-line override-loop duplicate. pm-3cbk remains open as the umbrella decision for further barrel/dedup work (jscpd still flags pm.history-redact <-> history-repair lock+ownership pattern as a candidate; extracting that needs a new src/core/history/history-rewrite.ts plus an uncovered-allowlist entry, deferred to keep this slice tight).
